---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 6: Segmentation, Detection, and Feature Extraction
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Segmentation, Detection, and Feature Extraction
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Build pixel/object-level analysis pipelines.

## 03. Recap from Unit 5
- DL baselines and data constraints.

## 04. Task taxonomy
- Classification vs detection vs segmentation.

## 05. Annotation formats overview
- Boxes, masks, keypoints.

## 06. Label quality and uncertainty
- Inter-annotator effects.

## 07. Semantic vs instance segmentation
- Output granularity differences.

## 08. Detection objective
- Localization + classification.

## 09. Feature extraction objective
- Quantitative descriptors for science.

## 10. U-Net intuition
- Encoder-decoder structure.

## 11. Detection architectures (conceptual)
- Two-stage vs one-stage.

## 12. Post-processing pipeline
- NMS and connected components.

## 13. Classical preprocessing link
- Denoise/contrast before inference.

## 14. Data augmentation for segmentation
- Geometry-consistent transforms.

## 15. Losses for segmentation
- Cross-entropy, Dice, focal variants.

## 16. Losses for detection
- Classification + localization terms.

## 17. Imbalance handling
- Rare-object strategies.

## 18. Metrics for segmentation
- IoU, Dice, boundary metrics.

## 19. Metrics for detection
- Precision/recall and mAP intuition.

## 20. Calibration in dense prediction
- Confidence map reliability.

## 21. Error taxonomy
- Missed, merged, split, hallucinated.

## 22. Artifact-driven false positives
- Imaging physics pitfalls.

## 23. Domain shift across instruments
- Generalization constraints.

## 24. Small-data strategies
- Transfer learning and patching.

## 25. Weak supervision overview
- Scribbles/points/pseudo-labels.

## 26. Active labeling loops
- Prioritize uncertain regions.

## 27. Feature extraction after segmentation
- Morphology/statistics pipeline.

## 28. Structure-property linkage
- Features as downstream inputs.

## 29. Reproducibility checklist
- Dataset/version/seed discipline.

## 30. Runtime considerations
- Throughput for lab workflows.

## 31. Human-in-the-loop validation
- Expert correction loop.

## 32. Case sketch: grain segmentation
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Case sketch: defect detection
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 34. Case sketch: phase feature extraction
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 35. Failure mode #1
- Over-segmentation noise.

## 36. Failure mode #2
- Missed tiny objects.

## 37. Failure mode #3
- Shortcut learning from metadata.

## 38. Mitigation checklist
- Robust deployment steps.

## 39. Explainability hooks
- Saliency and uncertainty overlays.

## 40. Integration into process models
- Bridge to Unit 7.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: label schema
- Define annotation protocol.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: train segmentation baseline
- Minimal U-Net experiment.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: train detection baseline
- Minimal detector experiment.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: evaluate metrics
- IoU/mAP + error slices.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: feature pipeline
- Extract morphology descriptors.

## 46. Exercise scaffold: post-mortem
- Analyze top failure types.

## 47. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield review.

## 48. Summary
- Core takeaways.

## 49. Reading assignment
- Prepare process-structure-property modeling.

## 50. References + transition
- Unit 7 bridge.

::: {#refs}
:::
